title: PALS - Pediatric Advanced Life Support Exam Questions
description: Pediatric Advanced Life Support questions from NREMT database covering pediatric cardiac, pediatric resuscitation, and emergency pediatric care

questions.text: What is the compression-ventilation ratio for 2 rescuer child CPR?
What is the compression-ventilation ratio for 2-rescuer infant CPR?
What is the recommended depth of chest compression for an infant victim?
options: Atleast one fourth the depth of the chest, approximately 1 inches (2.5cm)
Atleast one third the depth of the chest, approximately 1.5 inches (4cm)
Atleast one half the depth of the chest, approximately 2 inches (5cm)
Atleast two third the depth of the chest, approximately 3 inches (8cm)

questions.text: What is the preferred technique for providing chest compression during 2-rescuer CPR for an infant?
What is the best action to relive severe chocking in a responsive infant?
options: Kneel behind the infant and perform abdominal thrusts
Give 2 breaths, reposioning the airway after each breath
Begin 5 cycles of back slaps, followed by 5 chest thrusts

questions.text: What should you observe when trying to observe if rescue breath for an infant victim are effective?
options: Visible rise of the stomach with each rescue breaths
Visible rise of the chest with each rescue breaths
Complete compression of the ventilation bag
Air leaking around the ventilation bag

questions.text: Epinephrine auto injector for infant or child contain the dose:
What should you do when you are administering breath by using bag-mask for a child who is not breathing, but does have a pulse?
options: Give breath at a rate of 1 breath every 3 to 5 seconds
Position the child on his or her stomach
Squeeze the bag as often as possible

questions.text: How often should you deliver ventilation for infant during respiratory arrest?
The mental status of unresponsive infant is typically checked by flicking the feet and:
Once a child loses approximately ___ of blood, his/her condition is considered serious and life threatening :
A toddler is a child who is from ___ years of age.
When assessing infants and younger children EMTs should start with the ___ of the body and finish with the ___
The dosage epinephrine auto injector for child is :
A good rule to remember when communicating with children is:
options: Standing above the child will help ease fear.
You may need to lie to them so they will cooperate with you.
There is no need to tell them what you are doing.
Always come down to the child's level, or even slightly below.

questions.text: Normal respiratory rate in infant is:
In comparison to an adult airway structure, a child has:
options: The narrowest portion of the airway at the cricoid ring
A lower larynx, located at C5 to C6

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the chest and lungs of a child is true?
options: The chest muscles are immature and can tire easily
The chest wall offers enhanced protection for thoracic organs
A child relies very little on the diaphragm for breathing

questions.text: Parent have the right to refuse treatment on behalf of the child:
You have delivered an infant who is not breathing. After suctioning and physical stimulation, there are no respirations. You should:
options: clamp and cut the umbilical cord
provide ventilations at a rate of 40 to 60/min
start chest compressions at a rate of 60/min

questions.text: After birth, you notice that the infant's face and trunk appear blue. This is called:
Premature infants are at a higher risk for:
As you are delivering an infant, you note that the cord is loosely wrapped around the infant's neck. You should:
options: Delay delivery as long as possible
Wrap the cord in warm moist dressings
Cut the cord and remove it from around the infant's neck
Gently slip the cord over the infant's head

questions.text: If the amniotic sac has not ruptured before the delivery of the infant's head you should:
options: Continue with the delivery; the sac does not present a problem
Have the mother pant and stop pushing; this is a true emergency
Puncture the sac and remove it from around the infant's head
Immediately cross clamp the cord and deliver the infant

questions.text: On 1-minute evaluation of a newborn infant, you note the infant has blue arms and legs, a pink trunk, and a heart rate of 120. He is crying loudly and moving all extremities. The patient sneezes when stimulated. This patient's APGAR score is:
When using a bulb syringe to suction an infant just delivered, which of the following should you suction first?
The assessment tool commonly used to evaluate newborns is the:
After delivering a baby, you should dry the baby completely. What should you do next?
options: Wrap the baby in warm blankets
Wrap the baby in sterile saline sheets
Cool the baby to stimulate crying

questions.text: After delivery, you see that the infant's face and trunk are cyanotic. The infant is crying and has a pulse of 158/min. You should:
options: cool the infant to increase its circulation
provide ventilations at a rate of 40/min
calm the infant to slow down its respirations
dry the infant and provide free-flow oxygen

questions.text: Where should the umbilical cord be cut?
options: As close to the baby as possible
Just before the position of the first clamp
As close to the placenta as possible

questions.text: You have suctioned, dried, and wrapped the newborn in a blanket. The baby is still not breathing. You should stimulate the baby by:
options: cooling it off with saline-soaked sheets
clamping and cutting the umbilical cord
flicking the soles of the feet and rubbing its back
holding it with its head slightly higher than its body

questions.text: What is considered a breech birth?
options: The baby's face is the presenting part
The baby's head is too large to be delivered
The baby's buttocks are the presenting part

questions.text: The newborn should be placed in which position until the umbilical cord is cut?
options: at the level of the vagina

questions.text: What care should you give a newborn that has a pulse of 90/min and respirations of 28/min?
options: Allow the mother to hold the baby to calm it
Administer free-flow oxygen at 10 to 15 liters per minute
Ventilate the baby with a bag-valve-mask (BVM) device at 60/min

questions.text: Pulmonary resuscitation is necessary for a newborn if:
options: the baby weighs more than 7 lb
the breathing rate is greater than 50/min

questions.text: A mother is delivering in her home. The baby's head is crowning. You should:
options: dry the newborn's head with a sterile dressing
administer free-flow oxygen to the newborn at 10 to 15 liters per minute
apply gentle pressure to the head with your fingers to prevent an explosive delivery
apply gentle pressure to the mother's abdomen to assist with delivery of the body

questions.text: Which of the following situations would indicate adequate breathing when you assess a newborn's breathing?
options: a delivery time of less than 1 hour
a newborn with a strong cry

questions.text: As you deliver a baby, you find the umbilical cord wrapped around its neck. You should:
options: slip the cord over the baby's head
gently pull on the baby's head to release the cord
apply pressure to the head to slow delivery and transport rapidly

questions.text: Thick meconium staining is most likely to result in:
Calculate an APGAR score for the following infant. Body is pink with acrocyanosis. Pulse is 88/min and respirations are absent. Infant does not cry or move when stimulated, and muscle tone is weak.

You have just delivered a baby boy. His appearance is pink and his pulse is 148/min with slow respirations. He has a weak cry when stimulated and resists attempts to straighten his legs. His APGAR score would be?
Care for a premature infant includes:
options: cooling the newborn to stimulate respirations
covering both eyes with moist, sterile dressings
withholding oxygen until arrival at the hospital

questions.text: Why is it important to keep a newborn warm while providing treatment?
options: A newborn has difficulty regulating body temperature
It is easier for you to provide treatment in a warm environment
Oxygen will flow from an oxygen source with little difficulty in a warm environment
The warm environment will make the newborn sleepy, making transport easier

questions.text: You have delivered a limp infant who is not breathing. You should first:
options: stimulate the infant by flicking the soles of the feet

questions.text: Capillary refill is considered most reliable in children less than ___ years of age.
